meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances
meta.project_subject: Setting up of PMU for CPGRAMS with 3 resource persons for a period beyond 30-9-25
meta.deliverables: Review and streamlining existing categorization/taxonomy and mapping across Central Ministries/Departments by end of November 2025. Review of root cause analysis as a time-bound 4-month process covering about 85 Ministries/Departments. Study of Public Grievances System for select Ministries/Departments and States/UTs to understand grievance flow and bottlenecks as a 4-month process. Prepare monthly reports on public grievances received on CPGRAMS by the 5th of each month (last acceptable date 10th). Publish Annual Report on CPGRAMS by 2nd/3rd week of December. Make weekly/monthly presentations regarding CPGRAMS review at Secretary level and higher authorities. Assist in publicity & communication strategy and prepare reports/presentations/IEC material. Assist in rollout of Next Gen CPGRAMS and Chatbot coordination. Any other work assigned by DARPG.

content.full_text: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions extends a Work Order to Quality Council of India (QCI) for setting up a Project Management Unit (PMU) with three members at a cost of Rs.30,24,000/- (excluding taxes) for 6 months from 01-10-2025 to 31-03-2026 or until implementation of NextGen CPGRAMS, whichever is earlier. The PMU will review and streamline categorization/taxonomy across Central Ministries, conduct root cause analysis reviews, study public grievance systems, prepare monthly reports on CPGRAMS grievances by 5th of each month, publish annual reports by December, make weekly/monthly presentations to Secretary level, assist in publicity strategy, support Next Gen CPGRAMS rollout, and handle any other assigned tasks. Payment milestones are 20% each for months 1-2 and 15% each for months 3-6.
